2026-03-12T08:01:07.7211337Z Triage complete for issue #57006. Here's a summary of what was done:
2026-03-12T08:01:07.7211881Z 
2026-03-12T08:01:08.2464923Z **Labels applied:** `Compute` (service area, #e99695), `bug`, `needs-team-triage` (in addition to the existing `Mgmt` label)
2026-03-12T08:01:08.2465711Z 
2026-03-12T08:01:08.2465854Z **Key findings:**
2026-03-12T08:01:09.1217100Z - This is a genuine bug filed by a repo member (`ArcturusZhang`) — no spam/customer-reported flags needed
2026-03-12T08:01:09.1225048Z - Root cause: `ManagementInputLibrary.DefaultApiVersion` uses `ApiVersions.Last()` which crashes when the top-level namespace has no API versions (multi-service combined clients like Compute)
2026-03-12T08:01:10.2748709Z - Fix scope: 3 provider files need to derive API version per-REST-client from operation parameters instead of using a global value
2026-03-12T08:01:10.4416092Z - CODEOWNERS owners are `@JoshLove-msft @m-nash @jorgerangel-msft @live1206 @ArcturusZhang` — no "Service Attention" label needed, but `needs-team-triage` was added since no one is assigned

Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. IMPORTANT: Comments are subject to validation constraints enforced by the MCP server - maximum 65536 characters for the complete comment (including footer which is added automatically), 10 mentions (@username), and 50 links. Exceeding these limits will result in an immediate error with specific guidance. NOTE: By default, this tool requires discussions:write permission. If your GitHub App lacks Discussions permission, set 'discussions: false' in the workflow's safe-outputs.add-comment configuration to exclude this permission. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation. CONSTRAINTS: The complete comment (your body text + automatically added footer) must not exceed 65536 characters total. Maximum 10 mentions (@username), maximum 50 links (http/https URLs). A footer (~200-500 characters) is automatically appended with workflow attribution, so leave adequate space. If these limits are exceeded, the tool call will fail with a detailed error message indicating which constraint was violated.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). If omitted, the tool auto-targets the issue, PR, or discussion that triggered this workflow. Auto-targeting only works for issue, pull_request, discussion, and comment event triggers — it does NOT work for schedule, workflow_dispatch, push, or workflow_run triggers. For those trigger types, always provide item_number explicitly, or the comment will be silently discarded.","type":"number"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["body"],"type":"object"}},{"name":"add_labels","description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 5 label(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the issue or PR that triggered this workflow. Only works for issue or pull_request event triggers. For schedule, workflow_dispatch, or other triggers, item_number is required — omitting it will silently skip the label operation.","type":"number"},"labels":{"description":"Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"type":"object"}},{"name":"missing_tool","description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"}},{"name":"noop","description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. 2026-03-12T08:01:25.0933662Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-12T07:59:17.351016179Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57006,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] DefaultApiVersion crashes on multi-service client (Compute migration blocked)\",\"body\":\"## Bug Description\\n\\n`ManagementInputLibrary.DefaultApiVersion` (line 147) calls `InputNamespace.ApiVersions.Last()` which throws `Sequence contains no elements` for multi-service clients.\\n\\n`csharp\\n// ManagementInputLibrary.cs:147\\ninternal string DefaultApiVersion =\\u0026gt; InputNamespace.ApiVersions.Last();\\n`\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\nThe Compute TypeSpec spec uses a combined client pattern:\\n\\n`\\tsp\\n@client({\\n  name: \\u0026#34;ComputeClient\\u0026#34;,\\n  service: [Compute, ComputeDisk, ComputeGallery, ComputeSku],\\n})\\nnamespace ComputeCombine;\\n`\\n\\nThis produces a code model where:\\n- **Top-level `ApiVersions` is empty** (the combined namespace has no versions)\\n- **Each child service has its own API version** embedded in operation parameters:\\n  - `Compute`: `2025-04-01`\\n  - `ComputeDisk`: `2025-01-02`\\n  - `ComputeGallery`: `2025-03-03`\\n  - `ComputeSku`: `2021-07-01`\\n- Otherwise the code model is complete: 51 child clients, 147 models, 159 enums, all operations properly mapped.\\n\\n## Design Issue\\n\\nHaving a **global** `DefaultApiVersion` is architecturally wrong for multi-service clients. Looking at existing generated code (e.g. KeyVault), the API version only flows to REST client constructors as a fallback:\\n\\n`csharp\\n// KeyVaultCollection.cs (generated)\\n_vaultsRestClient = new Vaults(_vaultsClientDiagnostics, Pipeline, Endpoint, \\n                               keyVaultApiVersion ?? \\u0026#34;2025-05-01\\u0026#34;);\\n`\\n\\nEach REST client already stores its own `_apiVersion` field. For multi-service clients like Compute, each REST client should receive its **service-specific** API version (derived from the operation\\u0026#39;s `apiVersion` parameter default), not a single global default.\\n\\n## Callers of DefaultApiVersion\\n\\nOnly 3 places in the generator use it:\\n1. `MockableResourceProvider.cs:116` — passes literal to REST client constructor\\n2. `ResourceClientProvider.cs:298` — `apiVersion.NullCoalesce(Literal(DefaultApiVersion))`\\n3. `ResourceCollectionClientProvider.cs:291` — same pattern\\n\\nAll 3 should derive the API version from the specific REST client\\u0026#39;s operations rather than a global value.\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\nThis blocks the `Azure.ResourceManager.Compute` TypeSpec migration. The code model is otherwise complete and ready for generation.\\n\\n## Reproduction\\n\\n1. Set up `tsp-location.yaml` pointing to `specification/compute/resource-manager/Microsoft.Compute/Compute`\\n2. Add `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` emitter to the spec\\u0026#39;s `tspconfig.yaml`\\n3. Run `dotnet build /t:GenerateCode`\\n4. Generator crashes with: `Sequence contains no elements` at `ManagementInputLibrary.get_DefaultApiVersion()`\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57006\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"ArcturusZhang\",\"id\":10554446,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/ArcturusZhang\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/10554446?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"labels\":[\"Mgmt\"],\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":1,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":1},\"created_at\":\"2026-03-12T07:57:02Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-12T07:57:02Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

Looking at existing generated code (e.g. KeyVault), the API version only flows to REST client constructors as a fallback:\\n\\n`csharp\\n// KeyVaultCollection.cs (generated)\\n_vaultsRestClient = new Vaults(_vaultsClientDiagnostics, Pipeline, Endpoint, \\n                               keyVaultApiVersion ?? \\\"2025-05-01\\\");\\n`\\n\\nEach REST client already stores its own `_apiVersion` field. For multi-service clients like Compute, each REST client should receive its **service-specific** API version (derived from the operation's `apiVersion` parameter default), not a single global default.\\n\\n## Callers of DefaultApiVersion\\n\\nOnly 3 places in the generator use it:\\n1. `MockableResourceProvider.cs:116` — passes literal to REST client constructor\\n2. `ResourceClientProvider.cs:298` — `apiVersion.NullCoalesce(Literal(DefaultApiVersion))`\\n3. `ResourceCollectionClientProvider.cs:291` — same pattern\\n\\nAll 3 should derive the API version from the specific REST client's operations rather than a global value.\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\nThis blocks the `Azure.ResourceManager.Compute` TypeSpec migration. 2026-03-12T08:01:25.1006450Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-12T08:00:15.546565079Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"safeoutputs","payload":{"id":3,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"result\":\"success\"}","type":"text"}],"isError":false}}}
2026-03-12T08:01:25.1030592Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-12T08:00:50.737095059Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\n**Summary:** This is a confirmed bug in the management plane SDK generator (`Azure.Generator.Management`) where `ManagementInputLibrary.DefaultApiVersion` calls `InputNamespace.ApiVersions.Last()`, which throws `InvalidOperationException: Sequence contains no elements` for multi-service combined clients (like Compute). The root cause is a flawed assumption that a single global API version exists for all clients, which doesn't hold for specs using the `@client({ service: [...] })` combined-client pattern.\n\n**Reporter:** Repository member `@ArcturusZhang` — not customer-reported.\n**Blocking:** `Azure.ResourceManager.Compute` TypeSpec migration.\n**Affected component:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/ManagementInputLibrary.cs` (line 147) and its 3 callers.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Root Cause Analysis\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe bug is in `ManagementInputLibrary.cs`:\n\n```csharp\n// Line 147 — crashes when ApiVersions is empty (multi-service combined clients)\ninternal string DefaultApiVersion =\u003e InputNamespace.ApiVersions.Last();\n```\n\nThe Compute TypeSpec spec uses a combined-client pattern:\n\n```typespec\n@client({\n  name: \"ComputeClient\",\n  service: [Compute, ComputeDisk, ComputeGallery, ComputeSku],\n})\nnamespace ComputeCombine;\n```\n\nIn this pattern:\n- The top-level `InputNamespace.ApiVersions` is **empty** (the aggregated namespace has no direct API versions).\n- API versions live **per sub-service**, surfaced through operation parameter defaults:\n  - `Compute` → `2025-04-01`\n  - `ComputeDisk` → `2025-01-02`\n  - `ComputeGallery` → `2025-03-03`\n  - `ComputeSku` → `2021-07-01`\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔧 Suggested Fix Approach\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThe `DefaultApiVersion` property needs to be removed or made per-REST-client. There are 3 call sites to update:\n\n| File | Line | Current Usage |\n|------|------|---------------|\n| `MockableResourceProvider.cs` | 116 | Passes as literal to REST client ctor |\n| `ResourceClientProvider.cs` | 298 | `apiVersion.NullCoalesce(Literal(DefaultApiVersion))` |\n| `ResourceCollectionClientProvider.cs` | 291 | Same pattern |\n\n**Recommended fix:** Each of these 3 providers already knows which REST client it's generating for. They should derive the API version from the REST client's own operations (look up the `apiVersion` constant/enum parameter default on any operation belonging to that client), rather than using a global value.\n\nA safe fallback chain:\n1. Try to get API version from the specific `InputClient`'s operations' `apiVersion` parameter default\n2. Fall back to `InputNamespace.ApiVersions.LastOrDefault()` for single-service specs (backward compat)\n3. If still null, throw a descriptive exception\n\n```csharp\n// Example helper\nprivate static string? GetApiVersionForClient(InputClient client)\n{\n    return client.Operations\n        .SelectMany(op =\u003e op.Parameters)\n        .FirstOrDefault(p =\u003e p.Name == \"apiVersion\" \u0026\u0026 p.DefaultValue != null)\n        ?.DefaultValue?.Value as string;\n}\n```\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🧪 Reproduction Steps\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n1. Clone the Compute TypeSpec spec from `azure-rest-api-specs`\n2. Set up `tsp-location.yaml` pointing to `specification/compute/resource-manager/Microsoft.Compute/Compute`\n3. Add `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt` emitter to the spec's `tspconfig.yaml`\n4. Run `dotnet build /t:GenerateCode`\n5. Generator crashes: `InvalidOperationException: Sequence contains no elements` at `ManagementInputLibrary.get_DefaultApiVersion()`\n\n**Quick unit test repro:** Create an `InputNamespace` with empty `ApiVersions` and multiple child `InputClient` instances with `apiVersion` parameters, then invoke `ManagementInputLibrary.DefaultApiVersion`.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e✅ Sub-task Checklist\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [ ] Remove/refactor `DefaultApiVersion` in `ManagementInputLibrary.cs` to avoid crashing on empty `ApiVersions`\n- [ ] Update `MockableResourceProvider.cs:116` to derive API version from the specific REST client's operations\n- [ ] Update `ResourceClientProvider.cs:298` similarly\n- [ ] Update `ResourceCollectionClientProvider.cs:291` similarly\n- [ ] Add a unit/integration test covering the multi-service combined-client scenario (empty top-level `ApiVersions`)\n- [ ] Validate Compute TypeSpec generation succeeds end-to-end after the fix\n- [ ] Run `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/eng/scripts/Generate.ps1` to regenerate test projects and ensure no regressions\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📚 Relevant Resources\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [ManagementInputLibrary.cs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/ManagementInputLibrary.cs) — line 147\n- [MockableResourceProvider.cs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/MockableResourceProvider.cs) — line 116\n- [TypeSpec `@client` decorator docs](https://typespec.io/docs/libraries/http/reference/decorators#@client) — combined client pattern\n- [http-client-csharp-mgmt generator README](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt)\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n**Owners (per CODEOWNERS):** @JoshLove-msft @m-nash @jorgerangel-msft @live1206 @ArcturusZhang\n","item_number":57006},"name":"add_comment"}}}
2026-03-12T08:01:25.1051492Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-12T08:00:50.742885379Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"safeoutputs","payload":{"error":{"code":-32602,"message":"E007: Comment contains 11 mentions, maximum is 10"},"id":4,"jsonrpc":"2.0"}}
2026-03-12T08:01:25.1075358Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-12T08:01:03.592924184Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\n**Summary:** This is a confirmed bug in the management plane SDK generator (`Azure.Generator.Management`) where `ManagementInputLibrary.DefaultApiVersion` calls `InputNamespace.ApiVersions.Last()`, which throws `InvalidOperationException: Sequence contains no elements` for multi-service combined clients (like Compute). There are 3 call sites to update:\n\n| File | Line | Current Usage |\n|------|------|---------------|\n| `MockableResourceProvider.cs` | 116 | Passes as literal to REST client ctor |\n| `ResourceClientProvider.cs` | 298 | `apiVersion.NullCoalesce(Literal(DefaultApiVersion))` |\n| `ResourceCollectionClientProvider.cs` | 291 | Same pattern |\n\n**Recommended fix:** Each of these 3 providers already knows which REST client it's generating for. They should derive the API version from the REST client's own operations (look up the `apiVersion` constant/enum parameter default on any operation belonging to that client), rather than using a global value.\n\nA safe fallback chain:\n1.
